LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE DOW CHEMICAL COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. WHAT DISTINGUISHES BOCKRATH FROM THIS CASE IS THE ALLEGATION THAT PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES ARE ATTRIBUTABLE TO A SINGLE TOXIN, ASBESTOS. IN ESSENCE, PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANTS KNEW OF THE DANGERS OF ASBESTOS, PLACED ASBESTOS AND/OR PRODUCTS IN COMMERCE WITH ASBESTOS, FAILED TO WARN PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFF WAS INJURED. DEFENDANT'S KNOWLEDGE AND PLAINTIFF'S LACK OF KNOWLEDGE MAKE TARMANN INAPPLICABLE TO THIS CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 12/26/07, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. IF DEFENDANTS LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. JUDGE: MARY E. WISS, CLERK: D.LOK, REPORTER: SUSAN LEE, #4280 (D206)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-17-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE DOW CHEMICAL COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: KATHERINE Y. WANG, ESQUIRE (KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP, 415.775.1206) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFFS. WILLIAM E. STEIMLE, ESQUIRE (FILICE BROWN EASSA & MCLEOD LLP, 510.444.3131) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. WHAT DISTINGUISHES BOCKRATH FROM THIS CASE IS THE ALLEGATION THAT PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES ARE ATTRIBUTABLE TO A SINGLE TOXIN, ASBESTOS. IN ESSENCE, PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANTS KNEW OF THE DANGERS OF ASBESTOS, PLACED ASBESTOS AND/OR PRODUCTS IN COMMERCE WITH ASBESTOS, FAILED TO WARN PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFF WAS INJURED. DEFENDANT'S KNOWLEDGE AND PLAINTIFF'S LACK OF KNOWLEDGE MAKE TARMANN INAPPLICABLE TO THIS CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/AP) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
